Feature:Add New Order 5s 6ms 5

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that a valid order is created correctly
So that the trade lifecycle begins with a clean, auditable order state

@smoke @lifecycle @add-order
Scenario:
QA submits a new equity buy order
962ms 10

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 445ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 31ms

And market data is live for symbol "AAPL" 65ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 14ms

Given no existing open orders for symbol "AAPL" < 1ms

When the QA submits a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00 273ms

Then an order ID is assigned immediately 1ms

And the order appears in the blotter with status "New" 8ms

And the side is "Buy", quantity is 100, and limit price is 150.00 46ms

And the audit trail records the submission with timestamp and user ID 75ms

@lifecycle @add-order
Scenario:
QA submits a new equity sell order
997ms 10

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 405ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 32ms

And market data is live for symbol "AAPL" 80ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 17ms

Given no existing open orders for symbol "AAPL" < 1ms

When the QA submits a sell order for 50 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 155.00 343ms

Then an order ID is assigned immediately < 1ms

And the order appears in the blotter with status "New" 8ms

And the side is "Sell", quantity is 50, and limit price is 155.00 42ms

And the audit trail records the submission with timestamp and user ID 64ms

@lifecycle @add-order
Scenario:
Order submission is rejected when quantity is zero
996ms 8

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 447ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 30ms

And market data is live for symbol "AAPL" 79ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 12ms

When the QA submits a buy order for 0 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00 414ms

Then the order is not created 3ms

And an error message is displayed explaining the invalid quantity 10ms

And no order ID is assigned < 1ms

@lifecycle @add-order
Scenario:
Order submission is rejected when price is negative
1s 27ms 8

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 423ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 28ms

And market data is live for symbol "AAPL" 189ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 134ms

When the QA submits a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price -1.00 236ms

Then the order is not created 3ms

And an error message is displayed explaining the invalid price 11ms

And no order ID is assigned < 1ms

@lifecycle @add-order
Scenario:
Order submission is rejected for an unknown symbol
1s 22ms 8

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 633ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 41ms

And market data is live for symbol "AAPL" 62ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

When the QA submits a buy order for 100 shares of "FAKESYM" at limit price 100.00 251ms

Then the order is not created 5ms

And an error message is displayed explaining the unknown instrument 17ms

And no order ID is assigned < 1ms

Feature:Modify Order 3s 904ms 4

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that an open order can be modified correctly
So that price and quantity changes are reflected with full chain integrity

@smoke @lifecycle @modify-order
Scenario:
QA modifies the price of an open order
1s 165ms 11

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 422ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 30ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given an open buy order exists for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00 239ms

And the order status is "New" 7ms

When the QA modifies the limit price to 152.00 193ms

Then the order is updated with the new limit price 152.00 16ms

And the order quantity remains 100 13ms

And the order status remains "New" 13ms

And the original order and the replacement are linked in order history 136ms

And the audit trail records the modify action with timestamp and user ID 82ms

@lifecycle @modify-order
Scenario:
QA modifies the quantity of an open order
1s 47ms 10

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 393ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 31ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 15ms

Given an open buy order exists for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00 226ms

And the order status is "New" 7ms

When the QA modifies the quantity to 200 190ms

Then the order is updated with the new quantity 200 14ms

And the limit price remains 150.00 12ms

And the order status remains "New" 12ms

And the original order and the replacement are linked in order history 144ms

@lifecycle @modify-order
Scenario:
Modify is rejected when order is already filled
749ms 8

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 413ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 28ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has status "Filled" 218ms

When the QA attempts to modify the limit price to 155.00 18ms

Then the modify request is rejected 18ms

And the order status remains "Filled" 10ms

And a clear rejection reason is shown to the QA user 30ms

@lifecycle @modify-order
Scenario:
Modify is rejected when order is already cancelled
940ms 8

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 446ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 29ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 10ms

Given a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has status "Canceled" 368ms

When the QA attempts to modify the limit price to 155.00 18ms

Then the modify request is rejected 21ms

And the order status remains "Canceled" 10ms

And a clear rejection reason is shown to the QA user 36ms

Feature:Cancel Order 3s 769ms 4

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that an open order can be cancelled cleanly
So that no unjustified executions occur after cancellation

@smoke @lifecycle @cancel-order
Scenario:
QA cancels an open order
1s 51ms 10

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 424ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 27ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 9ms

Given an open buy order exists for 200 shares of "MSFT" at limit price 300.00 208ms

And the order status is "New" 5ms

When the QA submits a cancel request for the order 143ms

Then the system acknowledges the cancel request 3ms

And the order status transitions to "Canceled" 5ms

And no fill execution is recorded after the cancel timestamp 145ms

And the audit trail records the cancel event with timestamp and user ID 77ms

@lifecycle @cancel-order
Scenario:
Cancel is rejected for an already-filled order
868ms 8

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 404ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 30ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 12ms

Given a buy order for 50 shares of "MSFT" has status "Filled" 343ms

When the QA attempts to cancel the filled order 17ms

Then the cancel request is rejected 16ms

And the order status remains "Filled" 11ms

And a clear rejection reason is shown to the QA user 31ms

@lifecycle @cancel-order
Scenario:
Cancel is rejected for an already-cancelled order
880ms 8

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 382ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 27ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 10ms

Given a buy order for 50 shares of "MSFT" has status "Canceled" 374ms

When the QA attempts to cancel the already-cancelled order 19ms

Then the cancel request is rejected 18ms

And the order status remains "Canceled" 13ms

And a clear rejection reason is shown to the QA user 34ms

@lifecycle @cancel-order
Scenario:
Cancel of a partially filled order leaves correct state
968ms 12

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 414ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 34ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given an open buy order exists for 500 shares of "MSFT" at limit price 300.00 222ms

And the order has been partially filled for 200 shares 20ms

And the order status is "Partially Filled" 7ms

When the QA submits a cancel request for the remaining quantity 115ms

Then the system acknowledges the cancel request 2ms

And the order status transitions to "Partially Filled and Canceled" 4ms

And the cumulative quantity (CumQty) remains 200 11ms

And the leaves quantity (LeavesQty) becomes 0 11ms

And the audit trail records the cancel event with timestamp and user ID 111ms

Feature:Order Reject 4s 543ms 6

I need to verify that rejected orders show meaningful rejection reasons
So that QA can distinguish business rule rejections from system errors

Feature:Partial Fill 5s 937ms 3

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that a partial execution updates order fields correctly
So that CumQty, LeavesQty, AvgPx, and status are all accurate

@smoke @lifecycle @partial-fill
Scenario:
Order receives a single partial fill
2s 32ms 12

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 397ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 30ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given an open buy order exists for 500 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00 237ms

And the order status is "New" 5ms

When a fill execution arrives for 200 shares at price 149.80 1s 20ms

Then the order status becomes "Partially Filled" 17ms

And the cumulative quantity (CumQty) is 200 37ms

And the leaves quantity (LeavesQty) is 300 21ms

And the average price (AvgPx) is 149.80 15ms

And the execution appears in order history with correct quantity and price 181ms

And the position is updated to reflect 200 shares acquired 56ms

@lifecycle @partial-fill
Scenario:
Order receives two sequential partial fills
3s 88ms 13

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 425ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 28ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 10ms

Given an open buy order exists for 500 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00 223ms

And the order status is "New" 8ms

When a fill execution arrives for 100 shares at price 149.50 1s 10ms

And a fill execution arrives for 150 shares at price 150.00 1s 17ms

Then the order status is "Partially Filled" 86ms

And the cumulative quantity (CumQty) is 250 12ms

And the leaves quantity (LeavesQty) is 250 11ms

And the average price (AvgPx) is 149.80 12ms

And both executions appear in order history 138ms

And the position reflects 250 shares acquired 102ms

@lifecycle @partial-fill
Scenario:
Partial fill does not allow modification of filled quantity
816ms 7

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 384ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 28ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 13ms

Given an open buy order for 500 shares of "AAPL" has been partially filled for 200 shares 212ms

When the QA attempts to modify the quantity to 150 119ms

Then the modify request is rejected 54ms

And a clear rejection reason explains the quantity is below the filled amount 3ms

Feature:Full Fill 6s 81ms 4

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that a fully filled order reaches the correct terminal state
So that CumQty, LeavesQty, status, and position are all accurate and the order is closed

@smoke @lifecycle @full-fill
Scenario:
Order is fully filled in a single execution
1s 817ms 12

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 387ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 28ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given an open buy order exists for 100 shares of "TSLA" at limit price 200.00 238ms

And the order status is "New" 6ms

When a fill execution arrives for 100 shares at price 199.50 1s 11ms

Then the order status becomes "Filled" 4ms

And the cumulative quantity (CumQty) is 100 13ms

And the leaves quantity (LeavesQty) is 0 12ms

And the average price (AvgPx) is 199.50 12ms

And the order is closed and no further modifications are possible 23ms

And the position reflects 100 shares of "TSLA" acquired at average price 199.50 67ms

@lifecycle @full-fill
Scenario:
Order is fully filled across two partial executions

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 376ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 26ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 10ms

Given an open buy order exists for 300 shares of "TSLA" at limit price 200.00 229ms

And the order status is "New" 6ms

When a fill execution arrives for 100 shares at price 199.00 1s 4ms

And a fill execution arrives for 200 shares at price 200.00 1s 22ms

Then the order status becomes "Filled" 6ms

And the cumulative quantity (CumQty) is 300 15ms

And the leaves quantity (LeavesQty) is 0 12ms

And the average price (AvgPx) is 199.67 11ms

And both executions appear in order history 152ms

And the position reflects 300 shares of "TSLA" acquired 64ms

Feature:Duplicate Execution Protection 4s 350ms 3

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that replayed execution reports are safely ignored
So that positions and order state are not double-counted after FIX session recovery

@smoke @lifecycle @duplicate-exec
Scenario:
Replayed execution report is safely ignored
1s 494ms 11

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 373ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 27ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 10ms

Given a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled 212ms

And the fill execution has ExecID "EXEC-001" 140ms

When the exchange simulator replays the same execution with ExecID "EXEC-001" 518ms

Then the duplicate execution is detected and rejected 25ms

And the order status remains "Filled" 18ms

And the cumulative quantity (CumQty) remains 100 12ms

And only one execution record exists in order history for ExecID "EXEC-001" 92ms

And the position is not double-counted 62ms

@lifecycle @duplicate-exec
Scenario:
Duplicate partial fill is safely ignored
1s 416ms 10

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 409ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 28ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given an open buy order exists for 500 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00 213ms

And a fill execution with ExecID "EXEC-002" has already been processed for 200 shares 10ms

When the exchange simulator replays the same execution with ExecID "EXEC-002" 510ms

Then the duplicate is detected and ignored 37ms

And the cumulative quantity (CumQty) remains 200 23ms

And the order status remains "Partially Filled" 20ms

And only one execution record exists for ExecID "EXEC-002" 150ms

@lifecycle @duplicate-exec
Scenario:
Duplicate detection is logged for audit purposes
1s 440ms 8

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 379ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 29ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled 211ms

And the fill execution has ExecID "EXEC-003" 157ms

When the exchange simulator replays the same execution with ExecID "EXEC-003" 508ms

Then the duplicate rejection event is recorded in the application log 134ms

And the log entry contains the ExecID "EXEC-003" and the order ID 6ms

Feature:Overfill Protection 5s 459ms 3

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that fill quantities exceeding the order quantity are blocked
So that positions and books are never overstated

@smoke @lifecycle @overfill
Scenario:
Overfill attempt is blocked
1s 897ms 11

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 397ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 27ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 10ms

Given an open buy order exists for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00 254ms

And the order status is "New" 6ms

When a fill execution arrives for 150 shares at price 150.00 1s 17ms

Then the overfill is detected by the system 21ms

And the order quantity ceiling is enforced 26ms

And the cumulative quantity (CumQty) does not exceed 100 17ms

And an overfill alert or error is visible to the QA user 15ms

And the overfill event is recorded in the application log 103ms

@lifecycle @overfill
Scenario:
Overfill after partial fill is blocked
1s 879ms 10

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 410ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 30ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given an open buy order exists for 200 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00 215ms

And a fill execution has already been processed for 150 shares 11ms

And the order status is "Partially Filled" 3ms

When a fill execution arrives for 100 shares at price 150.00 1s 19ms

Then the overfill is detected by the system 24ms

And the cumulative quantity (CumQty) does not exceed 200 25ms

And the overfill event is recorded in the application log 127ms

@lifecycle @overfill
Scenario:
Exact fill quantity equal to order quantity is accepted
1s 682ms 8

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 374ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 27ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 10ms

Given an open buy order exists for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00 212ms

When a fill execution arrives for 100 shares at price 150.00 1s 9ms

Then the order status becomes "Filled" 11ms

And the cumulative quantity (CumQty) is 100 27ms

And no overfill alert is raised 8ms

Feature:Settlement Report 3s 470ms 4

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that filled trades appear correctly in the settlement report
So that settlement instructions are accurate and the T+2 cycle is respected

@smoke @lifecycle @settlement
Scenario:
Filled trade appears correctly in the settlement report
892ms 9

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 377ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 30ms

And today's trade date is a valid settlement business day 1ms

Given a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00 229ms

When the settlement report is generated or refreshed 128ms

Then the trade appears in the settlement report 6ms

And the report row contains: 77ms
field expected_value
symbol AAPL
side Buy
quantity 100
price 150.00
account QA_TEST_ACCOUNT

And the trade date matches today's date 19ms

And the settlement date is trade date plus 2 business days 21ms

Feature:RHUB Validation 3s 115ms 3

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that RHUB records match source trade details exactly
So that reconciliation breaks are caught before they reach downstream systems

@smoke @lifecycle @rhub
Scenario:
RHUB record matches the source trade
1s 345ms 12

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 395ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 28ms

Given a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00 233ms

And the trade appears correctly in the settlement report 139ms

When the RHUB processing run has completed 67ms

Then a RHUB record exists for the trade 86ms

And the RHUB symbol matches "AAPL" 9ms

And the RHUB quantity matches 100 7ms

And the RHUB price matches 150.00 8ms

And the RHUB account matches "QA_TEST_ACCOUNT" 9ms

And the RHUB settlement date matches the settlement report settlement date 177ms

And no reconciliation break is flagged for this trade 184ms

@lifecycle @rhub
Scenario:
No RHUB record exists for a cancelled order
918ms 5

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 388ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 27ms

Given a buy order for 200 shares of "AAPL" has been cancelled before any fill 371ms

When the RHUB processing run has completed 67ms

Then no RHUB record exists for the cancelled order 62ms

@lifecycle @rhub
Scenario:
RHUB reconciliation break is raised for a mismatched quantity
851ms 7

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 387ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 27ms

Given a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00 235ms

And the RHUB record for the trade has an incorrect quantity of 50 2ms

When the QA checks the RHUB reconciliation status 177ms

Then a reconciliation break is flagged for the trade 7ms

And the break details show the expected quantity 100 and actual quantity 50 12ms

Feature:Order History 3s 489ms 4

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that order history captures every lifecycle event in sequence
So that any defect can be diagnosed from the audit trail

@smoke @lifecycle @history
Scenario:
Order history captures a simple new and fill sequence
805ms 10

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 393ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 27ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 10ms

Given a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00 201ms

When the QA opens the order history for the order 73ms

Then the history contains exactly 2 events in sequence 8ms

And event 1 has type "New" with quantity 100 and price 150.00 34ms

And event 2 has type "Filled" with cumulative quantity 100 22ms

And each event has a timestamp that is later than the previous event 14ms

And each event records the user ID of the actor 18ms

@lifecycle @history
Scenario:
Order history captures the full add, modify, fill sequence
1s 65ms 9

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 407ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 28ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 9ms

Given the following actions have been performed on a single order: 446ms
action detail
New Buy 100 AAPL @ 150.00
Modify Price changed to 152.00
Filled 100 shares filled at 151.90

When the QA opens the order history for the order 98ms

Then the history contains exactly 3 events in correct sequence 8ms

And the events appear in the order: New, Replace, Filled 18ms

And each event has a timestamp that is later than the previous event 19ms

And each event records the user ID of the actor 26ms

@lifecycle @history
Scenario:
Order history captures partial fill then cancel sequence
896ms 8

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 378ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 29ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 12ms

Given the following actions have been performed on a single order: 354ms
action detail
New Buy 500 AAPL @ 150.00
Partial Fill 200 shares filled at 149.80
Cancel Remaining 300 shares cancelled

When the QA opens the order history for the order 79ms

Then the history contains exactly 3 events in correct sequence 10ms

And the events appear in the order: New, Partially Filled, Canceled 20ms

And the final status shown is "Partially Filled and Canceled" 12ms

@lifecycle @history
Scenario:
Order history is read-only and cannot be edited
721ms 6

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 395ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 26ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00 203ms

When the QA opens the order history for the order 74ms

Then no edit or delete controls are available on any history event 10ms

Feature:Position Management 4s 599ms 5

As a QA analyst
I need to verify that positions reflect only executed trade quantities
So that the position book is accurate and free from phantom or duplicate entries

@smoke @lifecycle @position
Scenario:
Position reflects only executed quantities
1s 409ms 10

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 438ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 27ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given the starting position for "AAPL" in account "QA_TEST_ACCOUNT" is 0 shares 126ms

When a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" is fully filled at 150.00 222ms

And a buy order for 50 shares of "AAPL" is partially filled for 30 shares 182ms

And a buy order for 200 shares of "AAPL" is submitted and then cancelled before any fill 323ms

Then the net position for "AAPL" is 130 shares 75ms

And the position does not include quantity from the cancelled order < 1ms

And the position does not include the unfilled 20 shares from the partial order < 1ms

@lifecycle @position
Scenario:
Rejected order has no position impact
771ms 7

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 374ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 29ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given the starting position for "MSFT" in account "QA_TEST_ACCOUNT" is 0 shares 82ms

When a buy order for 100 shares of "MSFT" is submitted with an invalid price 167ms

And the order is rejected by the system 50ms

Then the net position for "MSFT" remains 0 shares 54ms

@lifecycle @position
Scenario:
Sell order reduces position correctly
859ms 6

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 389ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 29ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 10ms

Given the starting position for "AAPL" in account "QA_TEST_ACCOUNT" is 200 shares 111ms

When a sell order for 50 shares of "AAPL" is fully filled at 155.00 244ms

Then the net position for "AAPL" is 150 shares 74ms

@lifecycle @position
Scenario:
Position updates are visible immediately after fill
793ms 6

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 395ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 29ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given the starting position for "TSLA" in account "QA_TEST_ACCOUNT" is 0 shares 80ms

When a buy order for 100 shares of "TSLA" is fully filled at 200.00 218ms

Then the net position for "TSLA" is 100 shares within 5 seconds of the fill 58ms

@lifecycle @position
Scenario:
Position management shows no duplicate entries
765ms 7

Given the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment 376ms

And the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION" 27ms

And the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders 11ms

Given a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00 197ms

When the QA views the position detail for "AAPL" 118ms

Then each execution appears exactly once in the position detail 12ms

And the total position quantity equals the sum of all unique fill quantities 22ms

Feature:Edge Case Scenarios 6s 870ms 9

As a QA engineer
I want to validate resilience and boundary conditions
So that the system behaves correctly under non-ideal circumstances

@known-issue @edge-case @session
Scenario:
Order submitted while FIX session is disconnected
775ms 2 1 2 1

Given the QA user is logged in 482ms

And the FIX session is forcibly disconnected 107ms Show Error + 
                                            
 Error: [KNOWN ISSUE] Mock has no real FIX session — session state cannot be controlled programmatically.
Fix needed: Integrate a FIX engine (e.g. QuickFIX/J) with controllable session state via admin API.

Scenario:
Session drops after order send but before acknowledgment

When the FIX session drops immediately after order submission 88ms Show Error + 
                                            
 Error: [KNOWN ISSUE] Mock processes orders synchronously in-memory. Mid-flight disconnect cannot be simulated.
Fix needed: Async order pipeline with durable message queue (e.g. RabbitMQ) and reconnect/replay logic.

 Error: [KNOWN ISSUE] Mock has no FIX sequence number management or message replay deduplication.
Fix needed: FIX sequence gap detection, resend request handling, and exec ID deduplication at session layer.

@known-issue @edge-case @race-condition
Scenario:
Cancel request crosses with fill in flight
1s 93ms 2 2 1

Given an open buy order exists for 500 shares of TSLA at 200.00 785ms

When a cancel request is sent simultaneously with a fill for 500 shares 87ms Show Error + 
                                            
 Error: [KNOWN ISSUE] Mock processes state changes synchronously — a true race condition between cancel and fill (500 shares) cannot be reproduced.
Fix needed: Async event bus with configurable message ordering and delay injection for race condition simulation.

@known-issue @edge-case @race-condition
Scenario:
Modify request arrives after order is fully filled
1s 258ms 2 1 1 1

Given an order for 100 shares of AAPL at 150.00 has been fully filled 1s 13ms

When a modify request is sent to change quantity to 200 shares 80ms Show Error + 
                                            
 Error: [KNOWN ISSUE] Mock does not enforce post-fill state immutability at the message routing layer for qty=200.
Fix needed: State machine guard at message ingestion point rejecting modifies on terminal-state orders.

@known-issue @edge-case @multi-user
Scenario:
Two users modify the same order simultaneously
316ms 1 5 1

Given user "trader_1" has an open order for 300 shares of MSFT at 390.00 130ms Show Error + 
                                            
 Error: [KNOWN ISSUE] Mock is single-session only. Cannot create orders for user "trader_1" (300 MSFT @ 390) in a separate session.
Fix needed: Shared backend store (Node.js server + WebSocket) so multiple browser sessions share state.

Screenshot +

Screenshot

And user "trader_2" is viewing the same order < 1ms

When "trader_1" modifies the quantity to 400 shares < 1ms

And "trader_2" simultaneously modifies the price to 395.00 < 1ms

Then only one modification should be accepted < 1ms

And both users should see the same final order state < 1ms

See screenshot above for UI state at time of failure.

@known-issue @edge-case @multi-user
Scenario:
User attempts action beyond their entitlement
1s 78ms 1 3 1

Given a read-only user "viewer_1" is logged in 169ms Show Error + 
                                            
 Error: [KNOWN ISSUE] Mock grants all permissions to any logged-in user. Cannot log in as restricted user "viewer_1".
Fix needed: Role-based entitlement model with per-user permission map enforced at login and action level.

@known-issue @edge-case @batch
Scenario:
Settlement report does not include trades after batch cutoff
232ms 1 3 1

Given a trade was executed at 16:01 ET after market close 119ms Show Error + 
                                            
 Error: [KNOWN ISSUE] Mock has no concept of market hours or trade timestamps (16:01 ET).
Fix needed: Time-aware order processing with configurable market session windows and trade timestamp enforcement.

@known-issue @edge-case @batch
Scenario:
RHUB processes before settlement is complete
209ms 1 3 1

Given a trade has been executed but settlement batch has not completed 91ms Show Error + 
                                            
 Error: [KNOWN ISSUE] Mock has no processing order dependency between settlement and RHUB.
Fix needed: Event-driven pipeline with a settlement-complete signal gating RHUB processing.

Screenshot

When RHUB processing runs before settlement finalizes < 1ms

Then RHUB should either wait or surface an "incomplete data" warning < 1ms

And the reconciliation status should not show "Completed" prematurely < 1ms
